tasks.label: Monitoring
tasks.summary: Scout fields frequently for pests, diseases, and deficiencies.
tasks.detail: Walk through your field in a zigzag pattern and check upper and lower leaf surfaces. Note early spots, curling, chewing damage, and unusual discoloration. Early detection saves cost and yield.

tasks.label: Site Selection
tasks.summary: Choose a field with good drainage and sunlight.
tasks.detail: Avoid low-lying patches that remain waterlogged. Prefer a site with good airflow and access to irrigation. Rotate away from last season's host crops to reduce disease carryover.

tasks.label: Field Preparation
tasks.summary: Prepare soil and beds for uniform stand.
tasks.detail: Incorporate compost, correct pH if needed, and create level beds. Break clods and remove old crop residue to reduce pest shelter. Align rows for irrigation efficiency.

tasks.label: Weeding
tasks.summary: Keep weeds low to reduce competition and pest hosts.
tasks.detail: Weed during early crop stage to protect yield potential. Weeds can harbor pests and diseases. Combine manual weeding with mulching where possible.

tasks.label: Irrigation
tasks.summary: Irrigate on schedule, avoid leaf wetness at night.
tasks.detail: Prefer drip or morning irrigation to reduce humidity stress and fungal risk. Avoid overwatering. Check soil moisture before each cycle.

tasks.label: Fertilization
tasks.summary: Apply balanced nutrients as per stage.
tasks.detail: Base dose on soil test. Split nitrogen where needed to avoid excess vegetative growth (which increases disease risk). Ensure potassium for stronger plant defense.

tasks.label: Preventive Measure
tasks.summary: Prevent before cure with hygiene and scouting.
tasks.detail: Sanitize tools, remove infected leaves, and avoid moving diseased debris across fields. Keep spacing for airflow. Use resistant varieties whenever possible.

stages.label: Seedling Stage
items: Maintain gentle irrigation and avoid waterlogging.
Scout for early chewing pests and damping-off symptoms.
Use clean trays and healthy seedlings for transplant.

stages.label: Vegetative Stage
items: Keep weeds under control to reduce competition.
Monitor leaf color for nitrogen deficiency/excess.
Improve airflow by maintaining spacing and pruning where applicable.

stages.label: Flowering Stage
items: Avoid moisture stress to improve flower retention.
Increase scouting frequency for pests and fungal outbreaks.
Follow label instructions for any spray near flowering.

stages.label: Harvesting Stage
items: Harvest at correct maturity for best quality.
Avoid bruising and keep produce shaded after harvest.
Separate damaged produce to reduce rot spread.

stages.label: Post Harvest
items: Store produce in cool, dry, ventilated conditions.
Plan crop rotation and residue management for next season.
